news.title: Swiggy to shift headquarters to Whitefield as Bellandur lease expires
news.para: Food-delivery platform Swiggy is relocating its headquarters from Bellandur to Whitefield within the next couple of months. The move aims to offer employees better connectivity, including proximity to two Metro stations. This decision stems from the expiry of their Bellandur lease, not infrastructure concerns.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government offers stamp duty concession for lease agreements
news.para: Uttar Pradesh's stamp and registration department is offering significant concessions on stamp charges for lease agreements up to 10 years. This move aims to encourage registration of rent agreements, which are currently often unregistered due to high costs, thereby reducing disputes and ensuring legal validity.

news.title: Maharashtra panel approve bid for Kamathipura redevelopment project
news.para: The state government's High Powered Committee has approved the Kamathipura Redevelopment Project, with Mhada as the nodal agency. This initiative aims to redevelop 800 old buildings spread across 34 acres, providing tenants and landlords with new two BHK flats. The project follows the successful redevelopment of BDD chawls.

news.title: Odisha RERA revises quarterly report formats from real estate agents
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Regulatory Authority (ORERA) has mandated a revised format for quarterly progress reports from registered agents to enhance accountability and streamline disclosures. This move follows an audit observation highlighting widespread non-compliance and insufficient data in agent filings. The updated proforma requires detailed transaction-level information, aiming to plug data gaps and ensure transparency in the property market.

news.title: Four out of 16 developers shortlisted for railways’ South Mumbai plot development
news.para: The Railway Land Development Authority has shortlisted four developers for a prime 2.5-acre plot in Mahalaxmi, South Mumbai. This project has a massive revenue potential of Rs 10,000 crore. The e-bidding process saw intense competition from top real estate firms.

news.title: Delhi court sends Ocean Seven Buildtech's director to 14-day ED custody in PMAY scam case
news.para: A Gurugram builder, Swaraj Singh Yadav, has been taken into 14 days custody by the Enforcement Directorate. This action is part of a money-laundering investigation linked to the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana scheme. Yadav is accused of cancelling PMAY flat allotments and reselling them at higher prices.

news.title: China's new home prices fall at fastest pace in a year in October 2025
news.para: In October, China's new home prices experienced their most significant monthly decline in a year, underlining the ongoing struggles within the property market. With authorities striving to regain stability, experts are advocating for more lenient mortgage policies to encourage investment. Meanwhile, trends show that both property investment and sales are still on the downturn.

news.title: UK's Land Securities lifts annual profit and rental income forecasts
news.para: Property company Land Securities has seen a slowdown in inquiries. Despite this, Landsec has raised its rental income growth outlook for 2026. The company is shifting investments towards retail and residential projects.

news.title: Odisha government to simplify land management with new law
news.para: Odisha is set to introduce a comprehensive Land Management Act, replacing outdated revenue laws to simplify land record processes for citizens. The government will also amend the Survey and Settlement Act, empowering tehsildars. A report on Puri's specific land challenges is due by December end to address departmental management issues and resolve disputes.

news.title: Pune: 1.3 lakh property owners barred from PMC’s tax amnesty scheme over repeat defaults
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation has banned repeat property tax defaulters from its amnesty scheme, which offers a 75% penalty discount. This move follows citizen group objections that some owners repeatedly exploit the scheme. The civic body aims to ensure fairness and financial discipline, as outstanding taxes, including penalties, amount to over Rs 13,000 crore.

news.title: Mysuru civic body to intensify ward-wise e-khata issuance drive
news.para: Mysuru City Corporation is intensifying its e-khata property document issuance drive across all nine wards to achieve 100% coverage of 2,27,883 properties. So far, 61,117 e-khatas have been issued, with the drive set to resume next Friday after a temporary slowdown. Nodal officers are appointed to monitor progress, aiming for issuance within three to four days of application.

news.title: Unitech's loss narrows to ₹739 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Unitech  disclosed a net loss of Rs 738.72 crore for the recently concluded September quarter, a slight improvement from previous figures. Their total income climbed to Rs 151.01 crore year-on-year. However, the company is grappling with a severe cash squeeze, causing delays in projects, particularly in the Delhi-NCR region.

news.title: Pune: Residents, environmentalists raise concerns over RMC plant near Pashan Lake
news.para: Residents and environmentalists in Pune are raising concerns over a proposed ready-mix concrete plant near Pashan Lake. They fear particulate matter emissions will harm local and migratory birds, and potentially worsen the lake's existing pollution. Concerns also include respiratory diseases for nearby residents and adverse effects on the local ecosystem.

news.title: Hyderabad: HYDRAA faces 700 cases after saving 1,000 acres
news.para: Hyderabad's asset protection agency, HYDRAA, faces nearly 700 lawsuits after reclaiming public properties worth Rs 55,000 crore. Despite legal challenges and accusations of overstepping, officials remain confident in their mission to protect public and environmental assets, stating they operate within legal frameworks.

news.title: Kansai Nerolac Paints' profit rises 11% to ₹133.3 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In the September quarter, Kansai Nerolac Paints celebrated an 11.3% boost in net profit, totaling Rs 133.31 crore. Revenue from operations, however, stayed at Rs 1,954.18 crore, primarily impacted by the heavy rains that hindered the demand for decorative paints. On a brighter note, the construction chemicals sector thrived.

news.title: Hubtown's  profit rises to ₹32 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Hubtown Ltd saw its net profit surge by 65% to Rs 31.67 crore in the September quarter. This growth was driven by increased income and strong performance in its luxury and premium residential projects. The company anticipates adding over 5 million sq ft of developable area through proposed amalgamations.

news.title: Draft rules ease setback, basement & parking norms in Bengaluru master plan
news.para: Bengaluru's building regulations are set for significant changes under the Revised Master Plan 2015, aiming to simplify compliance for homeowners with smaller plots. The proposed amendments ease setback requirements, relax basement norms, and modernize parking provisions, with public objections invited within 30 days.

news.title: Aviom India Housing Finance promoter offers to settle dues as six entities put in bids
news.para: According to the proposal, there would be an upfront payment of Rs 350 crore while the accrued interest would be paid in 24 months after the initial 26 months of principal payment. Ilmi's plan also includes full repayment to operational creditors and employees amounting to Rs 2.9 crore.

news.title: Indore: Cops get cracking against violators of tenant and guest verification norms
news.para: Indore police are cracking down on those not following tenant and guest verification rules. Surprise checks were conducted on hotels and lodges. Fourteen people were booked for not providing required information. Authorities warn of legal action for violations. Citizens are urged to cooperate with police for city safety and to prevent crime.

news.title: Noida authority issues notice to ATS Homes for Sports City delay
news.para: Noida Authority has issued a notice to ATS Homes for lease violations and delays in the Sports City project. The developer owes Rs 2,746 crore in dues. Construction is incomplete, and sports facilities remain unbuilt. This action follows court directives and a CAG audit. ATS Homes disputes the Authority's claims, citing court orders.
